questions.question: What is meant by a political institution?
questions.answer: A political institution is an organization that helps in governing a country and making decisions.

questions.question: Why are institutions important in a democracy?
questions.answer: Institutions ensure that decisions are made according to rules and procedures.

questions.question: Who took the formal decision to implement the Mandal Commission recommendation?
questions.answer: The Union Cabinet took the formal decision.

questions.question: What was the main recommendation of the Mandal Commission?
questions.answer: Reservation of 27% of government jobs for socially and educationally backward classes.

questions.question: Who heads the government in India?
questions.answer: The Prime Minister heads the government.

questions.question: What is the main function of Parliament?
questions.answer: The main function of Parliament is to make laws for the country.

questions.question: Who elects the members of the Lok Sabha?
questions.answer: The people of India elect the members of Lok Sabha.

questions.question: What is the term of the Lok Sabha?
questions.answer: The term of the Lok Sabha is five years.

questions.question: Why is Rajya Sabha called a permanent house?
questions.answer: It is called permanent because it is never dissolved.

questions.question: Who appoints the Council of Ministers?
questions.answer: The President appoints the Council of Ministers on the advice of the Prime Minister.

questions.question: What is the political executive?
questions.answer: The political executive consists of elected leaders who take major policy decisions.

questions.question: Who are permanent executives?
questions.answer: Permanent executives are civil servants who help implement policies.

questions.question: What is the role of civil servants?
questions.answer: Civil servants assist ministers in implementing government policies.

questions.question: What is the Cabinet?
questions.answer: The Cabinet is a group of senior ministers who take major policy decisions.

questions.question: Who is the head of the state in India?
questions.answer: The President is the head of the state.

questions.question: How is the President of India elected?
questions.answer: The President is elected by elected MPs and MLAs.

questions.question: What is the role of the judiciary?
questions.answer: The judiciary interprets laws and settles disputes.

questions.question: Name the highest court in India.
questions.answer: The Supreme Court.

questions.question: What is judicial review?
questions.answer: Judicial review is the power of courts to examine laws and declare them unconstitutional.

questions.question: What is Public Interest Litigation?
questions.answer: It is a legal action filed in court for the protection of public interest.

questions.question: Which institution controls the money of the government?
questions.answer: Parliament controls the public money of the government.

questions.question: Which house has more power in money matters?
questions.answer: Lok Sabha has more power in money matters.

questions.question: What happens if Lok Sabha passes a no-confidence motion?
questions.answer: The Council of Ministers must resign.

questions.question: Who is the supreme commander of the armed forces in India?
questions.answer: The President of India.

questions.question: What does an independent judiciary mean?
questions.answer: It means the judiciary works independently from the executive and legislature.
